name: Docker Essentials
description: Test your knowledge on core Docker commands and container concepts.

questions.question: Which command is used to create an image from a Dockerfile?
How do you start a container and interact with its shell immediately?
Which command allows you to view the standard output (logs) of a container?
What is the flag used to run a container in the background (detached mode)?
Which command is used to run a new process (like /bin/bash) inside a running container?
How do you list all containers, including those that are currently stopped?
Which instruction in a Dockerfile specifies the base image for the build?
Which command is used to remove a specific Docker image from your local machine?
How do you map host port 8080 to container port 80?
Which command removes all unused containers, networks, and dangling images?
In a Dockerfile, which instruction sets the default command to be executed when the container starts?
Which of the following best describes the fundamental purpose of a Docker Volume?
options: It increases the read and write speed of the container's internal file system.
It provides a way to persist data independently of the container's lifecycle.
It allows multiple images to share the same read-only layers during the build.
It acts as a virtual memory extension for containers running heavy processes.
